name: Isabella I of Castile
trade_partners: Portugal; Italian states; New World colonies
economic_impact_summary: Sponsored transatlantic voyages initiating Atlantic trade expansion and Spanish colonial commerce.
trade_policy_keywords: colonial expansion; Atlantic voyages; royal patronage

name: Elizabeth I
major_trade_regions: Atlantic Ocean; North America; East Indies
trade_partners: Netherlands; Ottoman Empire; East India Company
economic_impact_summary: Strengthened maritime trade and authorized the East India Company; laying foundations of British global commerce.

name: Mary I of England
economic_impact_summary: Strengthened Anglo-Spanish alliances influencing European trade and diplomatic-commercial networks.
trade_policy_keywords: dynastic alliance; trade diplomacy; port access

name: Catherine de Medici
economic_impact_summary: Influenced political stability which maintained Mediterranean commerce and key alliances.
trade_policy_keywords: court patronage; alliance building; market stability

name: Christina of Sweden
economic_impact_summary: Consolidated Baltic trade influence and strengthened control over key northern European sea lanes.

name: Anne of Denmark
economic_impact_summary: Enhanced maritime alliances influencing Baltic security and North Sea commercial movement.
trade_policy_keywords: maritime alliance; port security; naval supply

name: Catherine I of Russia
economic_impact_summary: Expanded Baltic-facing commercial orientation supporting Russia’s European integration.

name: Kosem Sultan
economic_impact_summary: Influenced governance supporting strategic Mediterranean and overland commerce across imperial corridors.

name: Nur Jahan
economic_impact_summary: Exercised authority over trade permits and influenced Indo-Persian commercial expansion.
trade_policy_keywords: trade permits; court influence; luxury textile trade

name: Queen Kalinyamat
economic_impact_summary: Led naval expeditions and strengthened maritime networks resisting external control of sea trade.
trade_policy_keywords: naval expeditions; anti-monopoly resistance; maritime raids

name: Queen Nzinga
economic_impact_summary: Negotiated shifting alliances influencing Atlantic commerce and regional leverage in coastal trade.

name: Maria I of Portugal
economic_impact_summary: Maintained Atlantic colonial trade systems and reinforced Brazil-Europe economic ties.

name: Anna Jagiellon
economic_impact_summary: Supported stability linking Baltic export corridors with Central European production and Western demand.
trade_policy_keywords: grain exports; Baltic access; port privileges

name: Margaret of Austria
economic_impact_summary: Governed a key commercial hub influencing connections between Iberian and Northern European markets.
trade_policy_keywords: commercial governance; hub management; market regulation

name: Sophia Alekseyevna
economic_impact_summary: Expanded diplomatic-commercial relations preparing Russia for future Baltic-facing trade growth.
